project: PoC: Unified Query Pipeline for Analytics Engine Integration
description: Validate the end-to-end flow of PPL and SQL queries against a Parquet-backed index through the SQL/PPL plugin, with RelNode handoff to the analytics-plugin. See docs/plans/poc-option-b.md for full plan.

userStories.title: Create analytics-engine-stub Gradle submodule
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a new analytics-engine-stub/ Gradle submodule so that analytics engine SPI interfaces and mock implementations are clearly separated from SQL/PPL plugin code.
acceptanceCriteria: New analytics-engine-stub/ directory exists with build.gradle
Module is registered in settings.gradle
Module compiles with Calcite dependency
Build passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Copy SPI interfaces from analytics engine PoC
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the analytics engine SPI interfaces (EngineCapabilities, QueryPlanExecutor, SchemaProvider) copied into analytics-engine-stub/ so the SQL/PPL plugin can depend on them.
acceptanceCriteria: EngineCapabilities.java copied into analytics-engine-stub/ with correct package
QueryPlanExecutor.java copied into analytics-engine-stub/
SchemaProvider.java copied into analytics-engine-stub/
All interfaces compile without modification
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Copied 5 SPI interfaces from PoC: EngineContext, SchemaProvider, QueryPlanExecutor, AnalyticsBackEndPlugin, EngineBridge. Original repo had no EngineCapabilities.java — EngineContext and AnalyticsBackEndPlugin serve that role. Also fixed spotless licenseHeader escape bug in build.gradle.

userStories.title: Add query routing logic for Parquet indices in PPL REST handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the PPL REST handler to detect when a query targets a Parquet-backed index and route it to a new code path, so that Parquet queries can be handled differently from Lucene queries.
acceptanceCriteria: PPL REST handler extracts target index name from the query
A hardcoded flag or mock index setting determines Lucene vs Parquet
Parquet index queries enter a new code path (returns placeholder error or empty response for now)
Lucene index queries are completely unaffected — existing pipeline unchanged
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented ParquetIndexRouting utility class with extractIndexName() (regex-based) and isParquetIndex() (hardcoded set). RestPPLQueryAction routes Parquet queries to placeholder BAD_REQUEST error. Reusable by US-004.

userStories.title: Add query routing logic for Parquet indices in SQL REST handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the SQL REST handler to detect when a query targets a Parquet-backed index and route it to a new code path, same as the PPL handler.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL REST handler extracts target index name from the query
Same routing mechanism as PPL (shared logic where possible)
Parquet index queries enter the new code path
Lucene index queries are completely unaffected
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Moved ParquetIndexRouting from plugin/ to common/ module for cross-module sharing. Added extractIndexNameFromSql() for SQL FROM clause parsing. RestSqlAction routes Parquet queries to placeholder BAD_REQUEST error.

userStories.title: Wire Parquet path with stub response and JDBC formatting
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Parquet code path to return a valid JDBC-formatted response with hardcoded schema and rows, so that the end-to-end REST round-trip works before real RelNode logic is added.
acceptanceCriteria: POST _plugins/_ppl with a Parquet index query returns valid JDBC JSON response
POST _plugins/_sql with a Parquet index query returns valid JDBC JSON response
Response contains schema (column names and types) and datarows
Response format is indistinguishable from a Lucene query response in structure
Uses existing response formatter infrastructure (not custom formatting)
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: ParquetStubResponse utility in protocol/ module (not common/ due to circular dependency). PPL uses SimpleJsonResponseFormatter, SQL uses JdbcResponseFormatter. 4-column schema (timestamp/status/message/active) with 2 sample rows.

userStories.title: Add basic explain API support for Parquet path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the _explain endpoint to return a placeholder plan for Parquet queries, so that explain works end-to-end before real RelNode logic is added.
acceptanceCriteria: POST _plugins/_ppl/_explain with a Parquet index query returns a valid explain response
POST _plugins/_sql/_explain with a Parquet index query returns a valid explain response
Explain response contains a placeholder plan description
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added formatExplainAsJson() to ParquetStubResponse. PPL handler checks request.path().endsWith("/_explain"), SQL handler uses existing isExplainRequest(). Both return stub JSON explain response for Parquet indices.

userStories.title: Add integration test scaffold for Parquet query path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests in integ-test/ covering the full REST round-trip for Parquet queries (query, explain, error cases), so that subsequent phases can add real logic while keeping ITs green.
acceptanceCriteria: New IT class in integ-test/ for Parquet query path
IT covers: PPL query returns valid response, SQL query returns valid response
IT covers: PPL explain returns valid response, SQL explain returns valid response
IT covers: Lucene query still works (no regression)
All ITs pass with ./gradlew :integ-test:integTest
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: ParquetQueryIT in integ-test/ with 5 tests: PPL query, SQL query, PPL explain, SQL explain, Lucene regression. JDBC formatter uses legacyTypeName (STRING->keyword). No test data needed — stub routing intercepts by index name.

userStories.title: Implement mock Calcite Schema for Parquet index
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a mock Calcite Schema in analytics-engine-stub/ that defines a test Parquet index with representative column types, so that RelNode generation can be tested against it.
acceptanceCriteria: Mock Schema defines a test table with columns: string, long, timestamp (standard Calcite SqlTypeName), boolean
Schema uses standard Calcite types (TIMESTAMP, VARCHAR, BIGINT, BOOLEAN) — NOT OpenSearch UDTs
Schema can be registered in a Calcite FrameworkConfig
Unit test validates type mapping
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: ParquetTable (AbstractTable) with 4 nullable columns: message/VARCHAR, status/BIGINT, timestamp/TIMESTAMP, active/BOOLEAN. MockParquetSchemaProvider implements SchemaProvider, registers ParquetTable as 'parquet_index'. 3 unit tests validate schema. Standard Calcite SqlTypeName only — no OpenSearch UDTs.

userStories.title: Implement EngineCapabilities for mock Parquet adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an EngineCapabilities implementation that declares which operators and functions the mock Parquet adapter supports, so that pushdown decisions can be tested.
acceptanceCriteria: EngineCapabilities declares support for: LogicalFilter, LogicalProject, LogicalAggregate, LogicalSort
EngineCapabilities declares supported standard functions (basic math, string, comparison)
EngineCapabilities does NOT declare support for full-text search functions (match, match_phrase)
Unit test validates supportsOperator() and supportsAllFunctions() behavior
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented EngineCapabilities interface and ParquetEngineCapabilities mock. Supports LogicalFilter, LogicalProject, LogicalAggregate, LogicalSort operators. Supports math, comparison, string, and aggregate functions. Blocks full-text search functions (match, match_phrase, etc.). 7 unit tests passing.

userStories.title: Implement absorption rules for mock Parquet adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want absorption rules (filter, project, sort, aggregate pushdown) gated by EngineCapabilities, so that supported operators are pushed into the boundary scan node.
acceptanceCriteria: Absorption rules push filter, project, sort, aggregate into boundary scan when EngineCapabilities supports them
Unsupported operators remain as Calcite logical nodes above the boundary
Unit tests validate pushdown for each operator type
Unit test validates that unsupported operator stays above boundary
Build passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire PPL V3 RelNode generation with mock Parquet Schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PPL V3 queries to generate a Calcite RelNode using the mock Parquet Schema and absorption rules, replacing the stub response from Phase 2.
acceptanceCriteria: PPL query 'source = parquet_index | where status = 200 | fields timestamp, status, message' generates RelNode with filter + project absorbed
PPL query 'source = parquet_index | stats count() by status' generates RelNode with aggregate absorbed
PPL query 'source = parquet_index | sort timestamp | head 100' generates RelNode with sort + limit absorbed
Phase 2 ITs updated to use real RelNode generation and still pass
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Phase 4. Wire UnifiedQueryPlanner with mock Schema from analytics-engine-stub/.

userStories.title: Verify fail-fast for unsupported PPL operations on Parquet
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unsupported PPL operations to fail fast with a clear error when targeting a Parquet index, so that users get immediate feedback instead of silent incorrect results.
acceptanceCriteria: PPL query with match() on Parquet index returns clear error message (not Calcite in-memory fallback)
PPL query with function not in EngineCapabilities returns clear error message
Error messages indicate the operation is not supported for Parquet indices
IT added for fail-fast error cases
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Phase 4. Test queries: 'source = parquet_index | where match(message, "error")' and 'source = parquet_index | eval new_field = upper(message)' (if upper not in EngineCapabilities).

userStories.title: Verify datetime functions work without UDT interference on Parquet
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that PPL datetime functions work correctly with standard Calcite TIMESTAMP types and are not broken by PPL V3's custom UDT overrides.
acceptanceCriteria: PPL query 'source = parquet_index | eval hour = hour(timestamp) | where timestamp > "2024-01-01"' generates correct RelNode
PPL query 'source = parquet_index | stats count() by span(timestamp, 1h)' generates correct RelNode
No errors from UDT type mismatch (EXPR_TIMESTAMP vs standard TIMESTAMP)
ITs added for datetime queries
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Phase 4. Critical validation — PPL V3 overwrites all datetime functions with UDT-based implementations. Parquet schema uses native Calcite types.

userStories.title: Wire ANSI SQL RelNode generation with mock Parquet Schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ANSI SQL queries to generate a Calcite RelNode using Calcite's native SQL parser and the mock Parquet Schema.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL query 'SELECT timestamp, status FROM parquet_index WHERE status = 200' generates RelNode with filter + project absorbed
SQL query 'SELECT count(*) FROM parquet_index GROUP BY status' generates RelNode with aggregate absorbed
Datetime functions work correctly with standard Calcite types (same as PPL validation)
SQL ITs added to integ-test/ and pass
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Phase 5. Reference ANSI SQL PoC at https://github.com/dai-chen/sql-1/tree/poc/unified-sql-support for Calcite SqlParser integration pattern.

userStories.title: Verify search function handling in SQL on Parquet
userStories.description: As a developer, I want full-text search functions in SQL queries to be handled correctly — absorbed into boundary scan when supported, or fail-fast when not.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL query 'SELECT * FROM parquet_index WHERE match(message, "error")' fails fast with clear error on Parquet (no inverted index)
match_phrase registered as custom SqlBasicFunction in Calcite operator table
IT added for SQL search function handling
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Phase 5. Reference ANSI SQL PoC for match_phrase registration pattern.

userStories.title: Implement stub transport action for RelNode handoff
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a stub analytics-plugin transport action (test plugin in integ-test/) that accepts a serialized RelNode and returns mock results, so that the full round-trip can be validated.
acceptanceCriteria: Stub transport action registered in a test plugin in integ-test/
Transport action accepts a serialized RelNode and query parameters
Transport action returns hardcoded schema + rows (mock execution)
SQL/PPL plugin hands off the optimized RelNode via this transport action
Full round-trip works: REST → routing → RelNode → transport action → stub execution → response
All existing ITs updated to go through transport action path and still pass
Build passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Phase 6. Use Java serialization for RelNode in PoC. This is the final integration point.
